main_rule: The orchestrator MUST block development with ROLE_COVERAGE_BLOCKER unless the requested concrete part has exactly one valid primary specialist or helper role at the same granularity as the requested write scope.

definitions.concrete_part: The smallest explicit service, path, workflow, role-governance surface, runtime contract, or integration area that fully contains the requested write scope and is mapped by an exact plugin-owned catalog entry.
definitions.valid_specialist_role: A plugin-owned role with an existing TOML artifact, role_kind=specialist, primary_eligible=true, exact concrete_scope, bounded allowed_write_boundary, explicit trust_boundary, required_validations, developer_instructions, model, model_reasoning_effort, and sandbox_mode.
definitions.parent_role_insufficient: A parent, project-group, controller, or umbrella role may classify the request but cannot authorize edits for a child or mixed concrete surface because it is broader than the requested write scope.
definitions.broad_role_invalid: A role is invalid for primary ownership when it spans unrelated parts, is controller/reviewer-only, lacks a bounded write boundary, or can be selected only through fallback/prefix/group matching instead of an exact concrete mapping.
definitions.primary_role: The one plugin-owned specialist or helper role selected for the requested concrete part; supporting, security, QA, and reviewer roles can never replace it.
definitions.supporting_roles: Reviewer-only or QA/security sidecar roles attached after the primary specialist or helper role is selected when trust, secret, external, production, deploy, or quality risk flags exist.
definitions.implementation_handoff: The orchestrator-to-worker transition that allows product, platform, runtime, deploy, migration, or integration edits only after role routing and independent control audit pass.

blocker_cases.meaning: The requested part cannot be identified as a known concrete path, service, workflow, or area.
The requested concrete part is path-like or plausible but lacks an exact plugin-owned catalog mapping.
Only parent, group, controller, or umbrella coverage exists; child implementation remains unauthorized.
The catalog points to no role, a missing TOML artifact, or an invalid/malformed role artifact.
The selected role is broad, controller/reviewer-only, not primary eligible, or lacks bounded specialist shape.
More than one primary candidate owns the same concrete target or alias/write-root mapping.

selection_method: Identify the requested concrete part from path, service, workflow, remote, dependency edge, and requested behavior.

Resolve candidates from exact aliases or declared write roots only; broad fallback matching is forbidden. Exact workspace submodule-root aliases may route parent gitlink pointers only and must not widen child working-tree authority.
Reject parent or group matches as parent_only blockers.
Reject controller, reviewer, or broad roles as invalid_broad_role blockers.
If exactly one valid primary specialist or helper role remains, attach reviewer-only supporting roles for risk flags and continue to audit.
Before closing a ROLE_COVERAGE_BLOCKER packet, run the source freshness preflight and distinguish STALE_ROLE_GATE_SOURCE from true missing coverage.
If zero or multiple primary roles remain, return the exact ROLE_COVERAGE_BLOCKER packet.

role_development.unsafe_implementation_policy: route/audit remain nonzero and implementation_handoff_allowed=false until one exact primary specialist or helper role validates at the requested write granularity.

must_confirm: no product/runtime/deploy edits happened before role coverage
dev-core root and layer routes are exact
deprecated projects paths do not provide parent-scope implementation authority
Kubernetes .225 migration is blocked without truth contracts, read-only evidence, role coverage, and explicit operator approval
.226 Sentry and current infra runtime are future lanes only
plugin source-repo identity and workspace repo identity are parent-only classifiers, not implementation authority
source freshness preflight ran before blocker closeout
stale role-gate source is reported as STALE_ROLE_GATE_SOURCE with safe next action

methodology_items.title: Concrete part
methodology_items.rule: Define the concrete part as the smallest explicit plugin-owned surface that fully contains the requested write scope through exact aliases or declared write roots; broad roots alone do not authorize implementation.

methodology_items.title: Valid primary role
methodology_items.rule: A valid primary role must be plugin-owned, have a live TOML artifact, set role_kind=specialist or role_kind=helper, set primary_eligible=true, and declare bounded write scope, trust boundary, required validations, model, reasoning effort, sandbox mode, and developer instructions.

methodology_items.title: Why parent role is insufficient
methodology_items.rule: Parent, group, controller, or umbrella coverage is classification-only and cannot authorize a narrower child or mixed-scope write because it is broader than the requested write boundary.

methodology_items.title: When broad role is invalid
methodology_items.rule: If the candidate role is broad, mixed-scope, controller/reviewer-only, or reachable only through parent/group matching, return invalid_broad_role and require decomposition before any implementation handoff.

methodology_items.title: How to choose exactly one primary role
methodology_items.rule: Resolve candidates from exact aliases and declared write roots only, prefer the longest exact concrete match, reject group-only matches, and return ambiguous_owner when more than one primary candidate remains. Dev-core targets under /srv/bears/dev must use exact layer aliases or declared write roots; deprecated /srv/bears/projects paths are transitional sources only. Source repository identities and submodule remotes are classification-only and must not widen write authority beyond exact submodule paths. Exact workspace submodule-root aliases may classify parent gitlink pointers only when the catalog names that exact root; /srv/bears/kubernetes is a kubernetes_deploy_core repo-root route, while /srv/bears/plugins/bears remains parent gitlink pointer only.

methodology_items.title: When to add supporting/security/QA roles
methodology_items.rule: Attach supporting security or QA roles only after the primary role is selected, only for explicit risk flags, and only when the attached roles are reviewer-only rather than primary replacements.

methodology_items.title: Exact blocker packet
methodology_items.rule: Emit the exact ROLE_COVERAGE_BLOCKER packet with why_blocked, evidence_checked, blocked_edits, allowed_next_actions, required_role_shape, and decomposition_required whenever coverage is missing or invalid.

methodology_items.title: Automatic role development
methodology_items.rule: A ROLE_COVERAGE_BLOCKER packet must include role-development metadata for the role-development lane, owned by bears-platform-role-governor, and expose role-development-plan rerun proof while implementation_handoff_allowed=false until a new exact primary specialist or helper role validates.

methodology_items.title: Mandatory validators and tests
methodology_items.rule: Run the deterministic validator and test set that proves one-primary-role routing, blocker behavior, reviewer-only attachments, and implementation-handoff blocking before any implementation may continue.

methodology_items.title: Forward tests
methodology_items.rule: Keep forward tests for child-under-group blocking, alias/path drift, one-primary-role stability during catalog growth, and broad fallback rejection so regressions fail closed. Forward tests must include /srv/bears/dev, Kubernetes, Android emulator .225, Sentry .226, and The Ants new and legacy paths, plugin source-repo identity blocking, and root workspace repo identity blocking.

methodology_items.title: Independent control audit evidence
methodology_items.rule: Record independent control audit evidence that shows the audit command, implementation handoff state, blocker proofs, validation results, and no product/runtime/deploy edits before role coverage closed.

methodology_items.title: Source freshness preflight
methodology_items.rule: Before ROLE_COVERAGE_BLOCKER closeout, record current plugin checkout SHA, root gitlink SHA, and root origin/main plugin gitlink SHA; if the requested mapping exists only in newer merged plugin state, emit STALE_ROLE_GATE_SOURCE and a safe next action instead of ROLE_COVERAGE_BLOCKER.

must_record: selected concrete part and exactly one primary specialist or helper role
exact blocker reasons covered by validators and forward tests
no product/runtime/deploy edits before role coverage
source freshness preflight SHAs before blocker closeout

durable_artifact.with_spec_kit_packet: design.md in the feature or spec directory
durable_artifact.without_spec_kit_packet: bounded section in the narrowest plugin docs or planning issue until a feature directory exists

approved_skip.use: Only explicit operator approval may skip a required design artifact.

narrow_bugfix_skip.use: Only one exact-file bugfix without boundary, runtime, deploy, restricted-data, public behavior, or contract/proof/schema change may skip design; contract/proof/schema indicators require Spec Kit or a non-interface rationale accepted by the route-specific checklist.

source_freshness_preflight.required_before: Run after a route/audit packet would close with ROLE_COVERAGE_BLOCKER and before blocker closeout is reported to the operator.
source_freshness_preflight.mapping_check: If the requested mapping exists only in newer merged plugin state, classify the result as stale source instead of true missing role coverage.
safe_next_actions: switch to a clean root-sync worktree
source_freshness_preflight.exact_role_policy: Exact-role policy remains active; generic role substitution is forbidden even when source freshness is stale.

validation_command_policy.failure_classification_rule: If an absolute-path unittest command fails while the repo-relative command passes, report command_shape_failure, not test_assertion_failure.
validation_command_policy.handoff_rule: Role-gate subagent handoffs must use repo-relative unittest paths from the declared working_directory.
